decisions.briefing: Markets are flat today with both NASDAQ and S&P 500 down a mild 0.2%, showing no clear directional conviction. Cash is only $301.86 — below the $500 threshold needed to open new positions meaningfully. Current holdings look healthy: AMZN is the standout with a score of 95 and trading above its cost basis, no stop-losses appear threatened. Holding steady and letting existing positions run.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities drifted slightly lower on April 29 as investors remained cautious ahead of key earnings and economic data releases, with neither bulls nor bears commanding clear control. Sector rotation continued quietly, with defensive and value names holding up while speculative growth names faced mild selling pressure. Without a strong catalyst, the tape reflected a wait-and-see posture typical of late-earnings-season trading.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市於4月29日小幅收跌，投資人在重要財報公佈前持觀望態度，多空雙方均未能掌握主導優勢。市場悄然進行板塊輪動，防禦型及價值類股相對抗跌，而投機性成長股則承受輕微賣壓。缺乏明確催化劑的情況下，市場整體呈現財報季尾聲常見的靜待訊號格局。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance penalty a portfolio suffers when idle cash sits uninvested while the market rises. While holding cash provides flexibility and cushion against drawdowns, too much of it over time reduces overall returns — today we're keeping our small cash reserve rather than forcing a suboptimal trade, accepting minimal drag in exchange for optionality.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指投資組合中閒置現金未能投入市場，導致整體報酬率下滑的現象。持有現金雖能提供靈活性及抵禦回調的緩衝，但長期持有過多現金將拖低整體收益。今日我們選擇保留有限現金，而非勉強進場，以小幅機會成本換取未來靈活佈局的空間。
